core_mechanism: A critical-threshold fault-suppression scheme via recursively nested (concatenated) redundant encoding: whenever the perturbation strength stays below a sharp critical threshold, hierarchical encoding drives residual error toward zero, and the threshold is pushed higher by tailoring the encoding to a strong anisotropy (bias) in the perturbation channel.
brief_reason: The abstract's whole result is an 'accuracy threshold' below which fault-tolerant operation succeeds, and it is raised by a factor of five precisely by exploiting the asymmetry of the noise (dephasing dominating other error types), i.e. matching the redundant-encoding scheme to a strongly anisotropic perturbation.
